content: @John Hubbard @Joel Fernandes do you have a plan for the mmu v3 page tables cross over? I assume once hopper/blackwell is merged, then the mmu series will enable support before it lands?
So far the plan is to make everything work on all GPUs, in whatever order works :rolling_eyes::blush:

From a runtime standpoint, version 2 will be working out of the box, but version 3 will require some work. I made it as modular as possible, adding whatever support I could for version 3, but there is still some stuff missing, like pram window support for version 3, etc.
The page table walking code is also written in a way that handles both versions generically.
Joel should I try any of this out on Blackwell yet? Or too early?

John, I think it is still too early depending on the review and how things might change, so it might be better to wait it out till we start merging some patches. I can also try to help out on Blackwell memory management as well. But of course, if you have time, you're welcome to try it out. But I would wait.

I should start splitting the patches and sending out the earlier patches. I think I was planning to do that today since we have to rework the later patches for the locking anyway, which will take some time, but we could probably start merging some prerequisites.
it's just an ordering problem, if we land blackwell upstream first, then mmu with v2 only would stop blackwell from loading anymore

Dave do you think we could start with merging the DRM body code movement patches? I could rebase and send it. I'm concerned about merge conflicts, but I think there is no concern from anyone about that.
we should probably get the move buddy code changes in sooner, @Danilo Krummrich what tree do you think, misc-next might be best to avoid conflicts, but then you might need a backmerge
It depends on what you mean by "land Blackwell". As far as I know, we would only be going as far as GSP boot and then skipping the MMU init because V3 MMU is not fully supported with current patches.
currently it doesn't skip, it exits with ENOTSUPP and driver fails to load
Also, while Danilo is looking into it, perhaps also Clist abstraction which has been looked at by Gary, perhaps I could bug him for a review tag as well.

Dave Airlie said:

we should probably get the move buddy code changes in sooner, Danilo Krummrich what tree do you think, misc-next might be best to avoid conflicts, but then you might need a backmerge

Yeah, let's take it through misc-next, I can do a backmerge if needed.
Ah ok.. so we want that to allow driver to load but skip mmu?
I.e until ver3 mmu is in
or just fix mmu, but it's probably a functional regression to just not load anymore
Yea definitely supporting ver3 is the next step. But I’m wondering why that would block ver2. We could do it incrementally no? There are already 26 outstanding patches
Not that 26 is too large a number but..

@John Hubbard also when the driver fails to load on blackwell and I try to load it again, I get FSB
timeout
if you change it to be optional it's fine, but if the driver loads on blackwell, and then we merge something that stops it from loading on blackwell, I'd say that is bad planning :-)
Yea I’ll make sure it doesn’t prevent Blackwell loading :) thanks for the feedback ;-)
Dave, I expect Ampere and Ada also have that reload problem which we haven’t yet fixed, right?
maybe, but only my blackwell laptop was feeling bored enough to try :-)

Danilo Krummrich said:

Dave Airlie said:

we should probably get the move buddy code changes in sooner, Danilo Krummrich what tree do you think, misc-next might be best to avoid conflicts, but then you might need a backmerge

Yeah, let's take it through misc-next, I can do a backmerge if needed.

@Joel Fernandes Before we do so, can you please split this up in two patches. One that only moves drm_buddy.c to its new location (including the file name change) and another patch that does the API rename? This should lead to a much cleaner git history, since git can track the file move this way.

@Danilo Krummrich would it not be weird to move the files but not rename the api? Because then it would make the tree in an intermediate state where the buddy allocator is referring to drm terminology for the api (but present in a non drm gpu directory!). I am also not sure if it is very straight forward to make it build in this intermediate state. If your concern is conflicts, I can help resolve them without splitting, just let me know which tree to rebase against.

Conflicts are unrelated and building makes no difference either. But I guess you can do it all in one commit, just make sure to use git mv first and then apply the changes, so it doesn't mess with git blame, which I think the current patch does.
Yeah that’s how I did it. Will double check again before sending still. Thanks (will be sending out tomorrow, along with sending other separate prerequisite patch series).

Danilo Krummrich said:

Conflicts are unrelated and building makes no difference either. But I guess you can do it all in one commit, just make sure to use git mv first and then apply the changes, so it doesn't mess with git blame, which I think the current patch does.

I am not sure I understand the suggestion. Moves are not explicitly tracked by git and is detected through the changes.
Yeah, at a second glance we indeed need two commits if we want to preserve git blame for drivers/gpu/buddy.c.
